message:
security(workflows): shell-safe quoting in session-start cass prompt

## Why

HandleSessionStartContext::build_context_prompt embeds the user's
raw query text into a `ft robot cass search <query> --limit 3`
command line that is handed to the agent as a suggested next step.
The previous implementation used serde_json::to_string to JSON-encode
the query, then wrapped it in double quotes. Double quotes in POSIX
shells are *weak* quotes: `$(...)`, backticks, and `$VAR` still
expand inside them.

If an agent pasted the suggested command into a shell verbatim, a
query like `$(rm -rf ~); \`whoami\`` would execute the
subshell — the prompt builder was effectively constructing a shell
command-injection primitive out of untrusted pane content.

## Change

Rewrites quote_for_shell_command to use POSIX strong-quoting:

  1. Wrap the entire value in single quotes.
  2. Escape any embedded single quote as the standard `'\''` sequence
     (close the string, emit an escaped quote, reopen the string).

Single quotes disable *all* expansion inside the quoted span, so
$(...), backticks, $VAR, globbing, and history expansion are all
neutralized — a pasted command is guaranteed to pass the query
through verbatim.

## Tests

  - Updated the existing golden assertion so the expected "Try: …"
    line matches the new single-quoted form, including the
    `\\\"quoted\\\"` -> `"quoted"` simplification that falls out of
    dropping the JSON escape layer.
  - Added handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_
    neutralizes_shell_metachars: exercises `$(rm -rf ~); \`whoami\``
    and asserts the dangerous substrings never appear unquoted.
  - Added handle_session_start_context_build_prompt_
    escapes_embedded_single_quote: exercises `it's broken; $(id)`
    and asserts the '\\'' escape is emitted correctly so the
    resulting command is still a single shell token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
